name: openvino2onnx

metadata.name: openvino2onnx
metadata.summary: An Open Neural Network Exchange (ONNX) Optimization and Transformation Tool.

documentation:
# OPENVINO2ONNX
A simple tool to convert your IR XML to ONNX file.

Supported OpenVINO IR Version

- IRv10: openvino>=2020,<2023
- IRv11: openvino>=2023

## Usage

1. Install from PyPI
```shell
pip install openvino2onnx
```

2. Convert IR using CLI
```shell
openvino2onnx model.xml
```

functions.name: openvino2onnx.domain.intel.openvino.xml_frontend.openvino_xml_to_onnx_graph
documentation:
Convert OpenVINO IR .xml file to an equivalent ONNX graph.

Args:
    model_path (str | Path): Path to the OpenVINO IR.xml file.
    model_bin (str | Path, optional): Path to the OpenVINO IR.bin file.
        Defaults to None. If model_bin is None, the binary is to be found in the
        same directory as the xml file with the same name but with a.bin extension.

classes.name: openvino2onnx.domain.intel.openvino.passes.eliminate_useless_floor.EliminateUselessFloorRewriter
documentation:
OpenVINO has a bug that floor on integers, it should be removed from the graph.

Before:

    x(i64) -> Floor -> y(i64)

After:

    x(i64) -> (Identity) -> y(i64)

    model2 (onnx.ModelProto): The second ONNX model to be compared.
    input_maps (Dict[str, numpy.ndarray], optional): The input data to be used for
        the comparison. If not provided, the model will be run with random input
        data.

        to be used for the comparison. Defaults to "onnx".

Returns:
    Dict[str, float]: A dictionary containing the accuracy metrics.

functions.name: openvino2onnx.convert_graph
documentation:
Convert an ONNX model to OnnxGraph

Args:

: model (str | os.PathLike | ModelProto): path to the model or a loaded model.
  passes (Sequence[str], optional): Names of selected passes. Defaults to None.
  exclude (Sequence[str], optional): Names of excluded passes. Defaults to None.
  onnx_format (str, optional): The serialization format of model file.
  strict (bool, optional): Break if any pass goes wrong. Defaults to False.
  configs (dict, optional): Specify configuration for passes
  print_passes (bool, optional): Print the selected passes. Defaults to True.
  target_opset (int, optional): Target opset version for ONNX domain. Defaults

  > to `OPENVINO2ONNX_OPSET.version`.

Returns:

: OnnxGraph: converted graph

functions.name: openvino2onnx.convert
documentation:
Convert an ONNX model with default or given passes

Args:

: model (str | os.PathLike | ModelProto): path to the model or a loaded model.

classes.name: openvino2onnx.passes.transform.fuse_swish.FuseSwishRewriter
documentation:
Fuse sigmoid-mul into Swish activation function.

> Swish is also known as SiLU (Sigmoid-Weighted Linear Unit) and is defined as:

$$
Swish(x) = x \cdot \sigma(x)

where :math:`\sigma` is the sigmoid function.

Before:

    a->sigmoid->mul->b
     \__________/

After:

    a->swish(function)->b
$$

documentation:
Fuse Mish activation function.

> Mish is defined as:

$$
mish(x) = x \cdot tanh(softplus(x))

where :math:`softplus(x) = ln(1 + exp(x))`.

Before:

    a->softplus->tanh->mul->b
     \_________________/

After:

    a->mish(function)->b
$$

methods.name: openvino2onnx.passes.pattern.SingleNodePattern.with_inputs
documentation:
Match the node with specific number of inputs and names.

Example:

```
# a concat with 2 inputs
SingleNodePattern("Concat").with_inputs(2)

# an add with an input from node "X"
SingleNodePattern("Add").with_inputs(2, "X", None)
```

Args:

: num_inputs (int): number of inputs
  input_nodes (str): names of each input node, this is not order sensitive,

  > you can use `None` to match any input.

methods.name: openvino2onnx.passes.pattern.SingleNodePattern.with_outputs

# a split with 2 outputs
SingleNodePattern("Split").with_outputs(2)
```

Args:

: num_outputs (int): number of outputs
  output_nodes (str): names of each output node, this is not order sensitive,

  > you can use `None` to match any output.

```

modules.name: openvino2onnx.passes.quantize.canonicalize_negative_scale
documentation:
Copyright Wenyi Tang 2024-2025

:Author: Wenyi Tang
:Email: wenyitang@outlook.com

classes.name: openvino2onnx.passes.quantize.canonicalize_negative_scale.CanonicalizeNegativeScaleRewriter
documentation:
Negative scale leads to quantization overflow on some devices,
for negative scales, we should flip the sign and check whether quantized
value exceeds the range.

Before:

    0.16 (x) / -0.00125 (scale) = -128 (q)

After:

    0.15875 (x) / 0.00125 (scale) = 127 (q)

modules.name: openvino2onnx.passes.quantize.canonicalize_negative_zero_point
documentation:
Copyright Wenyi Tang 2024

:Author: Wenyi Tang
:Email: wenyitang@outlook.com

classes.name: openvino2onnx.passes.quantize.canonicalize_negative_zero_point.CanonicalizeNegativeZeroPointRewriter
documentation:
Negative zero point is not supported on NPU-NCE engine.
For negative zero point, we should increase the scale to enlarge the range
of the data.

Before:

    x [1, 5] (float32) / 0.01569 - 64 = qx [0, 255]
    (scale [0.01569], zero_point [-64])

After:

    x [1, 5] (float32) / 0.01960 - 0 = qx [0, 255]
    (scale [0.01960], zero_point [0])

classes.name: openvino2onnx.passes.quantize.merge_quantize_input.MergeQuantizeInputPass
documentation:
Merge QuantizeLinear into inputs and change input type to uint8.

Example:

```
Before:

    input{float32} - QuantizeLinear - DequantizeLinear

After:

    input{uint8} - DequantizeLinear
```

modules.name: openvino2onnx.passes.quantize.fuse_qdq
documentation:
Copyright Wenyi Tang 2024-2025

:Author: Wenyi Tang
:Email: wenyitang@outlook.com

classes.name: openvino2onnx.passes.quantize.fuse_qdq.FuseQConvRewriter
documentation:
Fuse Quantize-Dequantize to QLinearConv.

Before:

    DequantizeLinear -----
                          | (weight)
    DequantizeLinear -> Conv -> QuantizeLinear
                         /
    DequantizeLinear ___/  (bias, optional)

After:

    QLinearConv

Note:

    Bias typestr in QLinearConv must only be int32,

classes.name: openvino2onnx.passes.quantize.fuse_qdq.UnfuseQConvRewriter
documentation:
Unfuse QLinearConv to Quantize-Dequantize.

Before:

    QLinearConv

After:

    DequantizeLinear -----
                          | (weight)
    DequantizeLinear -> Conv -> QuantizeLinear
                         /
    DequantizeLinear ___/  (bias, optional)

classes.name: openvino2onnx.passes.quantize.fuse_qdq.UnfuseQMatMulRewriter
documentation:
Unfuse QLinearMatMul to Quantize-Dequantize.

Before:

    QLinearMatMul

After:

    DequantizeLinear -----
                          | (weight)
    DequantizeLinear -> MatMul -> QuantizeLinear
                         /
    DequantizeLinear ___/  (bias, optional)

Choose a proper backend to evaluate ONNX models.
1. ReferenceEvaluator: no extra dependency, but extremely slow.
2. OpenVINO runtime: best for Intel platforms.
3. OnnxRuntime: best compatibility and balanced performance.
